content:
# Numbers Chapter 36 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 194-195; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. pa_0142 | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_036_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_036_page_002.jpg`

---

**36:1** And the heads of the fathers of the clan of the sons of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from the clans of the sons of Joseph, drew near and spoke before Moses and before the leaders, the heads of the fathers of the sons of Israel.

**36:2** And they said, "YHWH commanded my lord to give the land as an inheritance by lot to the sons of Israel, and my lord was commanded by YHWH to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother to his daughters.

**36:3** And if they become wives to any of the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel, then their inheritance shall be taken away from the inheritance of our fathers and shall be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall belong, and from the lot of our inheritance it shall be taken away.

**36:4** And when the Jubilee of the sons of Israel shall be, then their inheritance shall be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall belong, and from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers their inheritance shall be taken away."

**36:5** And Moses commanded the sons of Israel according to the mouth of YHWH, saying, "The tribe of the sons of Joseph speaks rightly.

**36:6** This is the thing which YHWH has commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying: Let them become wives to whomever is good in their eyes; only to a clan of the tribe of their father shall they become wives.

**36:7** And the inheritance of the sons of Israel shall not turn about from tribe to tribe, for each one of the sons of Israel shall cling to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers.

**36:8** And every daughter possessing an inheritance from the tribes of the sons of Israel shall become a wife to one from the clan of the tribe of her father, so that the sons of Israel may possess, each one, the inheritance of his fathers.

**36:9** And no inheritance shall turn about from one tribe to another tribe, for each of the tribes of the sons of Israel shall cling to its own inheritance."

**36:10** As YHWH commanded Moses, so did the daughters of Zelophehad do.

**36:11** And Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, became wives to the sons of their uncles.

**36:12** To those from the clans of the sons of Manasseh, son of Joseph, they became wives, and their inheritance remained upon the tribe of the clan of their father.

**36:13** These are the commandments and the judgments which YHWH commanded by the hand of Moses to the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**On the final chapter of Numbers:** Numbers 36 concludes the book by returning to the case of Zelophehad's daughters, first raised in Numbers 27:1-11. The literary architecture is deliberate: the book's final legislative section addresses a tension between two legitimate legal claims -- the daughters' right to inherit (ch. 27) and the tribe's right to preserve its territorial integrity (ch. 36). YHWH's resolution through Moses preserves both principles: the daughters may marry whomever they choose, but only within their father's tribe.

**Verse 1 -- Genealogical chain:** All three translators correctly render the dense construct chain tracing the petitioners' lineage through Gilead, Machir, and Manasseh. The Leningrad Codex reads rashei ha'avot l'mishpachat b'nei Gil'ad clearly on page 001. PNEUMA's "ancestral households" for avot is interpretive; "fathers" preserves the Hebrew term.

**Verse 5 -- "according to the mouth of YHWH":** The Hebrew al-pi YHWH is an important formula in Numbers (cf. 3:16, 39; 4:37; 9:18, 20, 23; 10:13; 13:3; 33:2, 38). EMET renders it "according to the mouth of YHWH," which is the most literal; this is adopted for the consensus over LOGOS's "word" and PNEUMA's "at the word," as the pi ("mouth") image is theologically significant -- Moses speaks what comes from YHWH's own mouth.

**Verse 7 -- "cling" (davaq):** All three translators recognize the significance of this verb. EMET and LOGOS render it "cling," while PNEUMA uses "hold fast." The verb davaq is the same used in Genesis 2:24 for the man-wife bond. "Cling" is more literal and preserves this important intertextual resonance.

**Verse 11 -- Name order:** The Leningrad Codex clearly reads Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, Noah -- confirmed on page 002 of the manuscript. This differs from the order in Numbers 27:1. All three translators preserve the MT order. The Vaticanus confirms this sequence in Greek transliteration.

**Verse 13 -- The colophon:** This verse closes the entire book of Numbers. The phrase "the commandments and the judgments" (ha-mitsvot v'ha-mishpatim) encompasses all the legislative and juridical content delivered in the plains of Moab. The geographical formula "by the Jordan at Jericho" (al Yarden Yericho) places Israel at the threshold of the promised land. The Leningrad Codex marks the end of the book with the Masoretic summary note visible at the bottom of page 002.
